name: Flat Barbell Bench Press
form_cues: Keep your feet flat on the floor
Retract your scapula (pull shoulders back and down)
Touch the bar to your mid-chest gently, then press up
common_mistakes: Flaring elbows out too wide (90 degrees)
Bouncing the bar off the chest
Lifting hips off the bench

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
form_cues: Set bench to a high incline (approx 75-80 degrees)
Brace core and press dumbbells straight up until arms are straight
Lower dumbbells under control until they are about ear height
common_mistakes: Arching the lower back excessively
Banging dumbbells together at the top
Not using full range of motion

name: Lat Pulldown
form_cues: Pull the bar down to your upper chest
Lead the movement with your elbows
Control the ascent (eccentric phase)
common_mistakes: Leaning back too far and using momentum
Pulling the bar behind the neck
Shrugging shoulders up at the bottom of the pull

name: Barbell Squat
form_cues: Place bar across your traps/upper back
Hinge at the hips first, then bend knees to descend
Squat down until thighs are at least parallel to floor
Drive up through the mid-foot
common_mistakes: Allowing knees to cave inward (valgus collapse)
Lifting heels off the ground
Rounding the lower back at the bottom (butt wink)

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
form_cues: Set bench to 30-45 degree incline
Keep elbows tucked slightly (about 45 degrees relative to torso)
Press dumbbells up in a slight arc path
common_mistakes: Incline too steep (making it a shoulder press)
Allowing dumbbells to drift backward over the head
Short-changing the depth at the bottom

name: Romanian Deadlift (RDL)
form_cues: Hold bar with shoulder-width grip, feet hip-width apart
Push hips backward, keeping knees slightly bent but static
Lower bar close to legs until you feel deep stretch in hamstrings
Keep back flat and squeeze glutes to return to top
common_mistakes: Rounding the spine
Bending knees too much (turning it into a standard deadlift)
Letting the bar drift away from the shins/thighs

name: Tricep Pushdown
form_cues: Pin your elbows to the sides of your ribs
Extend arms down fully, squeezing the triceps at the bottom
Return the weight slowly without moving your elbows
common_mistakes: Allowing elbows to flare or drift forward
Using hips or bodyweight to push the cable down
Not extending to full elbow lockout

name: Bicep Dumbbell Curl
form_cues: Stand tall, brace core, elbows at your sides
Curl weights up, rotating palms forward and up
Lower weights under control to full extension
common_mistakes: Swinging the body to lift the weight
Lifting elbows forward to bring weights higher
Not extending the arm fully at the bottom
